---
title: "GeoDataAccess.jl"
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
using GeoDataAccess
using GeoDataAccess: Cache, MetaData, fetch_data, DataAccessPlan, fetch, name,
    all_sources, available_sources,
    OpenMeteoArchive, OpenMeteoForecast, NOAANCEI, NASAPower, TomorrowIO, VisualCrossing,
    USGSEarthquake, USGSWaterServices, OpenAQ, NASAFIRMS, EPAAQS
using Dates
import GeoInterface as GI
using JSON
```

**GeoDataAccess.jl** is a Julia package for collecting geospatial data from multiple
public APIs.  It provides a unified interface across weather, hydrology, seismology, air quality,
and more — with built-in caching and [GeoInterface.jl](https://github.com/JuliaGeo/GeoInterface.jl)
integration for flexible spatial queries.

## Data Sources

| Source | Type | Coverage | Resolution | API Key |
|--------|------|----------|------------|---------|
| [`OpenMeteoArchive`](@ref) | Raster (ERA5) | Global, 1940–present | Hourly/Daily, 25 km | No |
| [`OpenMeteoForecast`](@ref) | Raster | Global, 16-day forecast | Hourly/Daily, 9 km | No |
| [`NOAANCEI`](@ref) | Station-based | Global stations, 1763–present | Daily | No |
| [`NASAPower`](@ref) | Raster | Global, 1981–present | Daily, 55 km | No |
| [`TomorrowIO`](@ref) | Raster | Global, 2000–present | Hourly/Daily, 4 km | Yes |
| [`VisualCrossing`](@ref) | Raster | Global, ~50 years | Daily/Hourly, 1 km | Yes |
| [`USGSEarthquake`](@ref) | Event-based | Global catalog | Event-based | No |
| [`USGSWaterServices`](@ref) | Station-based | US, 1.5M+ sites | Daily/15-min | No |
| [`OpenAQ`](@ref) | Station-based | Global, 11K+ stations | Hourly/Daily | Yes |
| [`NASAFIRMS`](@ref) | Event-based | Global, near real-time | 375 m / 1 km | Yes |
| [`EPAAQS`](@ref) | Station-based | US stations | Hourly/Daily | Yes |
| `LandfireSource` | Raster | CONUS, AK, HI | 30 m | No (email) |

## Installation

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.add("GeoDataAccess")
```

## Quickstart

The core workflow is: create a `DataAccessPlan`, inspect it, then `fetch` to download the data
as JSON files.

```{julia}
using GeoDataAccess
using GeoDataAccess: DataAccessPlan, fetch, OpenMeteoArchive
using Dates

# Create a plan: hourly temperature and precipitation for NYC
plan = DataAccessPlan(OpenMeteoArchive(), (-74.0, 40.7),
    Date(2024, 7, 1), Date(2024, 7, 3);
    variables = [:temperature_2m, :precipitation],
    frequency = :hourly)
```

```{julia}
# Execute the plan — returns file paths to cached JSON
files = fetch(plan)
```

```{julia}
# Read the JSON data
using JSON
data = JSON.parsefile(files[1])
keys(data)
```

Or use the convenience function `fetch_data` which combines both steps:

```{julia}
using GeoDataAccess: fetch_data, OpenMeteoArchive

files = fetch_data(OpenMeteoArchive(), (-74.0, 40.7),
    Date(2024, 7, 1), Date(2024, 7, 3);
    variables = [:temperature_2m, :precipitation],
    frequency = :hourly)
```
